title: Customer Service Representative

rationales.taskAutomation: Routine account questions, order status, and FAQ-style troubleshooting are now handled end-to-end by AI chat and voice agents at professional quality; complex, emotional, or multi-product cases still need a person.
rationales.toolMaturity: LLM-based support chatbots and voice agents are mature, widely shipped products integrated directly into the standard customer-support stack.
rationales.adoption: Customer support deflection is a mainstream mid-2026 expectation — most consumer-facing companies now route a majority of tier-1 volume through AI before a human ever sees it.
rationales.agenticExposure: Tier-1 support is a textbook agent-ownable pipeline: read the ticket, look up the account, resolve or refund, close the loop, with escalation to a human only on failure.

tasksAtRisk: Answering routine account and billing questions
Live chat and email ticket responses
tasksGrowing: De-escalating angry or high-stakes customer situations
Reviewing and correcting AI chatbot handoffs
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4051 (Customer Service Representatives), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI chat and voice agents now close a majority of tier-1 tickets without a human touch, which is why routine customer service sits at High risk. The remaining seats go to reps who handle the escalations an agent bounces back — angry customers, ambiguous multi-product problems, and judgment calls the bot won't make.
